questions.question: What is a resource?
questions.answer: Anything that can be used to satisfy human needs.

questions.question: Name two types of resources based on origin.
Give one example of a biotic resource.
Give one example of an abiotic resource.
What are renewable resources?
questions.answer: Resources that can be replenished naturally.

questions.question: Give one example of a non-renewable resource.
What is sustainable development?
questions.answer: Development that meets present needs without harming future generations.

questions.question: Where was the Earth Summit held in 1992?
What is Agenda 21?
questions.answer: A global action plan for sustainable development.

questions.question: What is resource planning?
questions.answer: A strategy for proper use and management of resources.

questions.question: Name one state rich in mineral resources.
Name one cause of land degradation.
What is soil?
questions.answer: The top layer of the earth supporting plant growth.

questions.question: Name the most widespread soil in India.
Which soil is suitable for cotton cultivation?
Which soil forms due to heavy rainfall and leaching?
Which soil is found in desert regions?
What is soil erosion?
questions.answer: The removal of the top fertile layer of soil.

questions.question: Name one method of soil conservation.
What is contour ploughing?
questions.answer: Ploughing along contour lines to reduce soil erosion.

questions.question: What are shelter belts?
questions.answer: Rows of trees planted to reduce wind erosion.

questions.question: What are Khadar soils?
questions.answer: New alluvial soils deposited by rivers.

questions.question: Why is resource conservation important?
questions.answer: To ensure resources are available for future generations.
